diagrams.title: Frequency Division Multiplexing (FDM)
diagrams.description: Shows multiple signals transmitted simultaneously over different frequency bands. Each signal gets its own dedicated frequency channel, with guard bands between them to prevent interference. The diagram displays three input signals (S1, S2, S3) each occupying a separate frequency range, combined into a single composite signal on the transmission medium, then separated back into individual signals at the receiver.
elements: Three input signal sources (S1, S2, S3)
Labels for frequency ranges (f1, f2, f3)
diagrams.purpose: Explains how FDM divides the frequency spectrum into separate non-overlapping channels, allowing multiple signals to share the same physical medium simultaneously without interfering with each other.

diagrams.title: Time Division Multiplexing (TDM)
diagrams.description: Illustrates how multiple signals share the same frequency channel by taking turns in time. Shows a time axis divided into frames, each frame containing time slots assigned to different signals. The diagram displays input signals sampled at specific intervals, combined into a time-interleaved stream, transmitted, then separated back to their original signals based on time slot position.
elements: Three input signal sources (S1, S2, S3)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how TDM allocates specific time slots to each signal, rotating through them in a fixed sequence. This shows the concept of digital multiplexing where signals share bandwidth by taking turns rather than having dedicated frequency ranges.

diagrams.title: Wavelength Division Multiplexing (WDM)
diagrams.description: Shows how multiple optical signals at different wavelengths (colors) are combined onto a single optical fiber. Similar to FDM but operating at light wavelengths in fiber optic cables. The diagram displays multiple laser sources emitting different colored lights (wavelengths), a multiplexer combining them into one fiber, the single fiber carrying all wavelengths, and a demultiplexer separating them back to individual detectors.
elements: Multiple laser light sources (different colors: red, green, blue, etc.)
diagrams.purpose: Explains WDM for fiber optic networks, showing how different light wavelengths (colors) can travel simultaneously through one fiber, dramatically increasing bandwidth capacity. This is the optical equivalent of FDM used in high-speed networking.
